content: We can refactor the OpenClaw ACP autonomous agent framework to use a hybrid episodic-semantic node memory system, where each ACP session with an external coding harness is stored as an episodic node linked to semantic nodes representing common coding patterns, API schemas, and debugging strategies retrieved across sessions. This lets the OpenClaw agent dynamically pull contextually relevant semantic knowledge anchored to past concrete coding interaction episodes, rather than relying on generic unconnected memory retrievals.
source_topics: agent harness components ACP openclaw autonomous agent framework
trigger_topic: agent harness components ACP openclaw autonomous agent framework
